instructions:
This is the listening section of the MET. There are three parts in this section, with special directions for each part.
Please answer all of the questions. You will not be able to go back after moving on to the next one. If you do not know the answer, you may guess. Try to answer as many questions as possible.

parts.instructions:
In this part of the test, you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will hear a question about it. Choose the answer to the question.

The conversations and questions will not be repeated. Please listen carefully.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the bus?
options: It is late.
It stops in a different place.
It has broken down.
It already came.

questions.question: What will the woman probably do the following day?
options: Drive to the city
Watch the parade on television
Give the man's family a ride
Take the train to the city

questions.question: What does the woman hope to do?
options: Learn to cook
Bring lunch from home
Avoid bad weather
Try some new restaurants

questions.question: What will the students do tomorrow?
options: Call the professor for their grades
Turn in their papers
Make appointments to see the professor
Pick up their quizzes

questions.question: What does the woman most likely want to do?
options: Compare different phone models
Have her phone repaired
Receive a refund
Explain her problem

questions.question: How did the man feel when he heard the news?
options: Happy
Surprised
Embarrassed
Angry

questions.question: What does the woman imply about the man?
options: He should continue down the street.
He's going to be late.
He's going in the wrong direction.
He should take a bus.

questions.question: How does the man feel?
options: Nervous
Confident
Confused
Excited

questions.question: What is probably true about the company?
options: It has new owners.
It is expanding rapidly.
It has lost many staff recently.
It is having financial problems.

questions.question: What happened when the man went to university?
options: He took his instrument with him.
He stopped playing the piano.
He began playing a new instrument.
He signed up for music classes.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the article?
options: She read part of it earlier.
She doesn't remember what it was about.
She looked it over very quickly.
She won't read it for another few days.

questions.question: What kind of toy does the man want to buy?
options: Something simple
Something that plays music
Something cheap
Something educational

questions.question: What are the speakers discussing?
options: Their math exam
Their group project
Their homework
Their favorite class

questions.question: Why will the professor contact the class?
options: To cancel a class
To give them materials for homework
To explain a recent lecture
To complain about the bookstore

questions.question: What are the speakers mainly discussing?
options: An athlete they like
A team they play for
A game they watched
A sports team's potential

questions.question: What does the man describe as annoying?
options: Airports
Waiting for relatives
Snowy weather
Travel delays

questions.question: When should the man probably feel better?
options: In about a week
By this evening
In two or three days
On Friday

questions.question: What are the speakers mainly talking about?
options: Moving to a new apartment
Their plans for the summer
How hot the weather is
Fixing the air conditioner

questions.question: How does the man feel about the new policy?
options: He thinks it benefits all students.
He thinks it is a good idea.
He believes it will affect him negatively.
He believes it will make little difference.

parts.instructions:
In this part of the test, you will hear conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will answer some questions about it. Choose the answer to each question.

The conversations will not be repeated. Please listen carefully.

questions.question: What are the speakers mainly discussing?
options: The state of the man's business
The woman's new company
The seminar the man will attend
An event for business owners

questions.question: Why does the man mention next year?
options: He will start a new business then.
He will miss the conference then.
He thinks the economy will be better then.
He thinks his business may be closed then.

questions.question: What is the man likely to do next?
options: Call his office
Attend a seminar
Ask the woman to lunch
Drive back to his office

questions.question: What does the man mean when he says:
options: His business will close soon.
His business suffered some damage.
His business isn't yet ready to open.
His business may not make money.

questions.question: Why does the man tell the woman she's lucky?
options: The item she wants is on sale.
The store is open late today.
He has what she is looking for.
She is getting married.

questions.question: What problem does the woman have?
options: The store doesn't have her size.
Someone else bought what she wanted.
She doesn't like anything in the store.
She doesn't have enough money.

questions.question: What does the man mean when he says:
options: He will close the store early.
He will recommend another suit.
He will sell the suit to someone else.
He will reserve the suit temporarily.

questions.question: What are the speakers discussing?
options: A course textbook the woman is returning
A course textbook the woman needs
A book the woman had reserved online
A book the woman's friend recommended

questions.question: Why does the man ask about the woman's research?
options: To send her to the correct building
To suggest a topic for a project
To help her find what she needs
To learn more about her interests

questions.question: What will the woman probably do?
options: Get the electronic version of the book
Borrow the book from her friend
Check the book out from the library
Come back later to get the book

questions.question: What does the woman mean when she says:
options: The book didn't contain useful information.
The book wasn't available online.
She didn't know what to do.
She didn't find the name of the book.

questions.question: What did the woman do the previous semester?
options: Started to use technology while teaching
Trained her assistant to teach her course
Asked students about how they used laptops
Gathered evidence about students' use of laptops

questions.question: Why does the woman talk about research studies?
options: To explain how she got her idea
To argue against the man's opinion
To describe the type of work she enjoys
To explain what she'll discuss with her students

questions.question: What does the man think about the woman's idea?
options: It is better than his idea.
It might influence others.
It is already accepted at their school.
It might be rejected by the administration.

parts.instructions:
In this part of the test, you will hear some short talks. After each talk, you will answer some questions about it. Choose the answer to each question.

The talks will not be repeated. Please listen carefully.

questions.question: What is the talk mainly about?
options: The different types of birds in a park
The eating habits of a type of bird
The speaker's research on endangered species
The characteristics of a type of bird

questions.question: What does the speaker suggest the listeners do?
options: Use equipment to help them see better
Use equipment to climb a tree
Try to catch some fish
Try to take a photo of the bird

questions.question: What does the speaker think the bird standing on the nest is doing?
options: Waiting for its mate
Protecting eggs
Looking for food
Watching the tour group

questions.question: Why does the speaker mention fish?
options: To tell the listeners what they'll do next
To compare the behavior of different animals
To explain what one bird is doing
To describe what lives in the lake

questions.question: What is the speaker mainly discussing?
options: The schedule for a project
Principles of design
Different construction materials
Plans for a new building

questions.question: Why does the speaker mention a home appliance store?
options: To suggest an interesting storage method
To criticize a common sales strategy
To describe inexpensive display ideas
To compare different types of shoppers

questions.question: What will the meeting participants most likely do next?
options: Discuss the project's budget
Visit a store
View some drawings
Sign a contract

questions.question: What does the speaker suggest about his team when he says:
options: They had difficulty making a decision.
They were confused about the instructions.
They did not want to change their minds.
They had concerns about an idea.

questions.question: What is the talk mainly about?
options: Student activities at a high school
Academic programs at a high school
Teaching staff at a high school
An upcoming sporting event at a high school

questions.question: What does the speaker say about other teachers?
options: They help with the sports programs.
They teach a variety of subjects.
They will also talk to the parents.
They will take the parents on a tour.

questions.question: Why does the speaker mention some equipment?
options: To explain what parents will need to buy
To explain how students play sports safely
To explain a benefit of two sports teams
To explain a disadvantage of a sports team

questions.question: What does the speaker say about participating in a sport?
options: It can be dangerous.
It is required at this school.
It is not appropriate for all students.
It is a valuable experience.

questions.question: What will the audience probably do next?
options: Hear about music and art
Watch a basketball game
Listen to some students speak
Ask the speaker some questions

questions.question: What will the students be doing?
options: Making copies of ancient objects
Looking for artifacts in Greece
Selling some of the pottery they make
Volunteering at a local museum

questions.question: What does the speaker wish he could do?
options: Be a student at City College
Touch museum objects
Meet a famous artist
Own an ancient Greek bowl

questions.question: What is mentioned about the oven?
options: It is very old.
It was made in Greece.
It was made for this project.
It was designed by the students.

questions.question: What will happen in May at the museum?
options: There will be an exhibit of student pottery.
Students will teach a pottery class.
The museum will be temporarily closed.
Art scholars will give lectures about Greek pottery.
